CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, created_at) VALUES (1, LOCALTIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, created_at) VALUES (2, LOCALTIMESTAMP + INTERVAL '1 hour');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, created_at) VALUES (3, LOCALTIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '1 day');
SELECT id, created_at FROM test_table WHERE created_at = LOCALTIMESTAMP;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE created_at <= LOCALTIMESTAMP;
WITH current_time AS (SELECT LOCALTIMESTAMP AS now) SELECT * FROM current_time;
CREATE TABLE additional_table (ref_id INT, ref_time TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO additional_table (ref_id, ref_time) SELECT id, LOCALTIMESTAMP FROM test_table;
SELECT t.id, a.ref_time FROM test_table t JOIN additional_table a ON t.id = a.ref_id WHERE a.ref_time > LOCALTIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '2 hours';
SELECT t.id, (LOCALTIMESTAMP - t.created_at) AS time_difference FROM test_table t;
CREATE TABLE log_table (event VARCHAR(100), event_time TIMESTAMP DEFAULT LOCALTIMESTAMP);
SELECT * FROM log_table WHERE event_time >= LOCALTIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '1 week';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM log_table WHERE event_time BETWEEN LOCALTIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '1 month' AND LOCALTIMESTAMP;
